expectations.text: Test class extends KernelTestBase (NOT BrowserTestBase or WebDriverTestBase)
expectations.evidence: Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php line 5: 'use Drupal\KernelTests\KernelTestBase;' and line 17: 'class SocialAiIndexingServiceTest extends KernelTestBase' — correct base class for service testing.

expectations.text: Test class has protected static $modules array listing required modules
expectations.evidence: Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php lines 22-31: 'protected static $modules = [system, user, node, field, text, filter, social_ai_indexing, social_ai_indexing_test]' — comprehensive module list including all dependencies.
Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php line 25: 'node' is present. Line 29: 'social_ai_indexing' is present. Both required modules are in the $modules array.

Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php line 37: 'protected function setUp(): void' defined. Line 38: 'parent::setUp();' is the first statement in the setUp() method — correct ordering as the skill prescribes.

expectations.text: setUp() calls $this->installEntitySchema('node') or similar entity schema installation
expectations.evidence: Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php line 41: '$this->installEntitySchema(user)' and line 42: '$this->installEntitySchema(node)' — both required entity schemas installed in setUp().
Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php lines 12-15 (PHPDoc): '@group social_ai_indexing_test' annotation is present.

expectations.text: Test uses $this->container->get() or \Drupal::service() to load the service under test
expectations.evidence: Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php line 58: '$service = $this->container->get(social_ai_indexing.indexer)' — uses container injection (preferred in Kernel tests) rather than static facade.

expectations.text: Test uses proper assertion methods ($this->assertNotNull, $this->assertEquals, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php line 59: '$this->assertNotNull($service, ...)' and lines 74-75: '$this->assertNotNull($loaded_node, ...)' and '$this->assertEquals(topic, $loaded_node->getType(), ...)' — proper PHPUnit assertion methods used.

notes: social_ai_indexing module is fictional — test cannot actually run against a real service

expectations.text: Test class extends KernelTestBase (NOT BrowserTestBase or WebDriverTestBase)
expectations.evidence: Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php line 6: 'use Drupal\Tests\BrowserTestBase;' and line 13: 'class SocialAiIndexingServiceTest extends BrowserTestBase' — wrong base class. BrowserTestBase installs a full Drupal site and is 10-100x slower than KernelTestBase for service testing.

expectations.text: Test class has protected static $modules array listing required modules
expectations.evidence: Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php lines 18-21: 'protected static $modules = [node, social_ai_indexing]' — array is present, though incomplete (missing system, user, field, text, filter).
Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php line 19: 'node' is present. Line 20: 'social_ai_indexing' is present. Both required modules listed.

Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php has no setUp() method defined. The class has only testServiceIsAccessible() and testTopicNodeCreation() methods. Without setUp(), there is no parent::setUp() call and no entity schema installation.

expectations.text: setUp() calls $this->installEntitySchema('node') or similar entity schema installation
expectations.evidence: Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php has no setUp() method and therefore no installEntitySchema() call. In a KernelTestBase context this would cause 'Table not found' errors when node entities are queried.
Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php lines 9-11 (PHPDoc): '@group social_ai_indexing_test' annotation is present.

expectations.text: Test uses $this->container->get() or \Drupal::service() to load the service under test
expectations.evidence: Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php line 27: '$service = \Drupal::service(social_ai_indexing.indexer)' — uses static facade. While container injection ($this->container->get()) is preferred in Kernel tests, the assertion accepts either form and \Drupal::service() is listed as acceptable.

expectations.text: Test uses proper assertion methods ($this->assertNotNull, $this->assertEquals, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Output file SocialAiIndexingServiceTest.php line 28: '$this->assertNotNull($service, ...)' and line 43: '$this->assertEquals(topic, $loaded->getType())' — proper PHPUnit assertion methods used.

notes: BrowserTestBase with @group annotation in Kernel namespace path could cause test runner confusion
